toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if the test host process crashes. 

For .NET, crash dumps can be collected using environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables crash dump collection
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — full dump (1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full)
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` — path for the dump file

These environment variables tell the .NET runtime to automatically generate a crash dump when the process crashes.


Now I'll put together the command with these environment variables set.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-359a41ed028e4f53a04132b18d367d1b && \\\nDOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \\\nDOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \\\nDOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=\"/tmp/sv-359a41ed028e4f53a04132b18d367d1b/crashdump_%p.dmp\" \\\ndotnet test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's the command used:

```bash
DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="/tmp/sv-359a41ed028e4f53a04132b18d367d1b/crashdump_%p.dmp" \
dotnet test
```

**How it works:**

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` | Enables automatic crash dump generation |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` | Captures a **full** dump (use `1` for mini, `2` for heap, `3` for triage) |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=...` | Sets the output path; `%p` is replaced with the crashing process's PID |

If the test host crashes, a `.dmp` file will be written to the project directory. No crash occurred this run, so no dump was generated.
